message.content:
You are translating a React SPA (CitedBy.ai) to support i18n (FR/EN/ES). Your job is to replace ALL hardcoded French text in the following 5 pages with i18n translation keys.

PROJECT ROOT: C:/Users/User/citedby-ai

PATTERN TO FOLLOW:
1. At the top of each page file, add: `import { useTranslation } from '../i18n/context';`
2. Inside the component function, add: `const { t } = useTranslation();`
3. Replace every hardcoded French string with `t.sectionName.keyName`
4. For data arrays with French text, move them INSIDE the component so they can reference `t.*`
5. Fill the corresponding empty section in the 3 locale files with actual translations

FILES TO MODIFY:
- pages/SMCStudio.tsx → section `smcStudio` in locale files
- pages/SchemaOrg.tsx → section `schemaOrgPage` in locale files
- pages/RobotsTxt.tsx → section `robotsTxtPage` in locale files
- pages/LlmsTxtGenerator.tsx → section `llmsTxtPage` in locale files
- pages/BlogOptimizer.tsx → section `blogOptimizerPage` in locale files

LOCALE FILES (all at C:/Users/User/citedby-ai/i18n/locales/):
- fr.ts: Fill empty `smcStudio: {},` with `smcStudio: { title: '...', ... },` etc (French = original text)
- en.ts: Fill with English translations
- es.ts: Fill with Spanish translations

IMPORTANT RULES:
- Do NOT modify any files in services/, app/, lib/, supabase/
- Replace ALL visible French text (headings, labels, buttons, placeholders, descriptions, tabs, tooltips, error messages, empty states, step descriptions)
- Use existing `common` section keys for shared terms (t.common.back, t.common.save, t.common.generate, etc.)
- Use existing `generativeTools` section keys if some labels are already there
- Keep ALL existing functionality intact - only change the text rendering
- These are generative tool pages - they typically have forms with labels, step indicators, output sections

Read each page first, then make all changes. Work through all 5 pages systematically.
